---
name: 13.21-create-21-reusable-call-workflow
description: Create the 21 caller GitHub Actions workflow that invokes the local reusable title translation workflow with the Attention paper title.
---

# Create 21 Caller Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/21-caller-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate creating a second caller workflow instance that reuses the same called workflow with a different report title default.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `21-reusable-call-workflow` in `.github/workflows/21-caller-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce a second local reusable workflow invocation:

- Set a dynamic run name that includes `21-reusable-call-workflow` and the Git ref name.
- Trigger on any push.
- Support manual execution from the GitHub Actions UI with inputs for `report_title`, `source_language`, and `target_language`.
- Set the `report_title` default to `Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems: Attention is All You Need, 2027`.
- Do not append author names to the default title because the full title plus names exceeds the 128-character limit enforced by the called workflow.
- Document that the called workflow enforces the 128-character title limit.
- Configure `source_language` and `target_language` as choice inputs with `en`, `fr`, `de`, and `nl-BE` options.
- Document `en`, `fr`, and `de` as ISO 639-1 language codes, and `nl-BE` as Dutch plus the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 Belgium region code.
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
- Add a job named `warn-when-languages-match` that emits a GitHub Actions warning when source and target languages are the same.
- Add a job named `call-title-translator` that calls `./.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml` at the job level.
- Pass `report_title`, `source_language`, `target_language`, and `caller_workflow` into the called workflow.
- Quote the `report_title` expression passed to the called workflow because the fallback title contains a colon.
- Ensure the called workflow has deterministic translations configured for the Attention title and does not return the quick-brown-fox translation for workflow 21.
- Pass `caller_workflow` as `${{ github.workflow }}`.
- Add a job named `display-translated-title` that needs `call-title-translator` and prints the source title and translated title.

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow file path, workflow name, dynamic run name, trigger behavior, read-only permission, job names, local reusable workflow path, input names, and default values.
3. Keep title length validation in `.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml`; do not add a duplicate title validation job to the caller.
4. Do not add direct steps to the reusable workflow calling job because reusable workflows are called at the job-level boundary.
5. Keep this workflow focused on calling and displaying reusable workflow results; do not add secrets, artifacts, caches, service containers, or deployment behavior.
6. Use the local workflow call syntax for workflows in the same repository.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/21-caller-workflow.yml` and `.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml .github/workflows/21-caller-workflow.yml
}
```
